description: The CodeBoarding architecture is a multi-stage pipeline that transforms raw source code into high-level architectural insights. It orchestrates environment setup, performs static analysis and graph-based clustering, utilizes an agentic reasoning core for deep-dive code inspection, and generates final documentation and visual artifacts.

components.name: Environment & Orchestration
components.description: System entry point and lifecycle manager handling CLI parsing, environment bootstrapping, and pipeline coordination.

components.name: Execution Controller
components.description: Manages the primary entry point, CLI parsing, and the high-level selection of analysis pipelines.

components.name: Infrastructure Provisioner
components.description: Ensures the local environment has the necessary runtimes and Language Server Protocol (LSP) tools required for deep code analysis.

components.name: Source & State Manager
components.description: Handles the lifecycle of the target codebase, including cloning, local path resolution, and maintaining the analysis cache.

components.name: System Monitor & Validator
components.description: Executes health checks and architectural validations while collecting telemetry on the analysis process.

components.name: Extensibility Framework
components.description: Provides the registry pattern used by all other components to discover plugins, tools, and health check definitions.

components.name: Analysis Intelligence
components.description: Represents the logic that the Orchestration layer feeds into, consuming the prepared environment to generate architectural abstractions.

components.name: Static Analysis & Graph Engine
components.description: Language-agnostic engine that extracts symbols and call graphs, applying community detection to cluster code elements.

components.name: Language Protocol Interface
components.description: Manages the lifecycle and communication with external Language Servers, abstracting JSON-RPC messaging.

components.name: Semantic Normalization Layer
components.description: Translates language-specific syntax and package structures into a standardized internal format.

components.name: Call Graph & Symbol Engine
components.description: Constructs the system's Control Flow Graph by resolving symbol references and building directed edges.

components.name: Community Detection & Clustering
components.description: Applies graph-theory algorithms to detect tightly coupled code communities and build hierarchical structures.

components.name: Analysis Orchestrator
components.description: Coordinates the end-to-end analysis workflow and manages incremental updates to the graph.

components.name: Agent Abstraction Layer
components.description: Converts technical graph data into high-level snapshots and deltas optimized for LLM consumption.

components.name: Agentic Reasoning Core
components.description: AI-driven intelligence layer using multi-agent strategies to interpret code clusters and generate architectural patterns.

components.name: Agent Orchestrator
components.description: The central control plane that manages the lifecycle of architectural analysis using a Top-Down Reasoning pattern.

components.name: Prompt Strategy Engine
components.description: A model-agnostic interface using the Factory pattern to generate optimized prompts for various LLM providers.

components.name: Context & Budget Controller
components.description: Manages Tool-Augmented Generation constraints by calculating token budgets and pruning Control Flow Graphs.

components.name: Agentic Toolkit Interface
components.description: Provides tools for agents to interact with static analysis results and explore repository structures.

components.name: Semantic Insight Resolver
components.description: Maps fuzzy LLM references back to exact source code locations and structures architectural data models.

components.name: Repository Tooling
components.description: Provides secure, abstracted interfaces for agents to interact with the physical codebase, including file I/O and git change detection.

components.name: Agent Tooling Interface
components.description: Provides the primary API for AI agents to query and manipulate the repository, abstracting filesystem operations into tools while maintaining shared context.

components.name: Repository Guard & Discovery
components.description: Enforces repository boundaries by managing ignore patterns and identifying project-specific configurations to ensure agents only process relevant code.

components.name: Change Tracking & Git Operations
components.description: Interfaces with Git to detect modifications, parse diffs, and identify line-range changes for incremental analysis.

components.name: Analysis Cache Manager
components.description: Manages persistence of analysis results and file metadata using fingerprinting to optimize performance.

components_relations.relation: uses for filtering and scope validation

components.name: Documentation & Output
components.description: Presentation layer that transforms analysis models into human-readable formats like Mermaid diagrams and HTML/Markdown documentation.

components.name: Interactive Visualization Engine
components.description: Orchestrates the creation of dynamic, browser-based documentation using Cytoscape.js for interactive graph explorations.

components.name: Static Document Generator
components.description: Manages the transformation of analysis data into text-based formats like Markdown, MDX, and Sphinx, including Mermaid.js diagram generation.

components.name: Rendering & Formatting Core
components.description: Provides foundational logic for data normalization, identifier sanitization, and mapping internal IDs to human-readable labels.

components_relations.relation: Provides sanitized identifiers and ID-to-name mappings
Supplies string sanitization and Mermaid logic
Shares template fragments and structural metadata
